title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables

input_row.title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables
input_row.description: Kubernetes supports mounting secrets as data volumes or as environment variables. Minimize the use of environment variable secrets.
input_row.rationale: It is reasonably common for application code to log out its environment (particularly in the event of an error). This will include any secret values passed in as environment variables, so secrets can easily be exposed to any user or entity who has access to the logs.
input_row.audit: Information about ways to provide sensitive data to pods is included in the documentation. Providing sensitive data to pods Run the following command to find references to objects which use environment variables defined from secrets. oc get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A
input_row.remediation: If possible, rewrite application code to read secrets from mounted secret files, rather than from environment variables.
input_row.impact: Application code which expects to read secrets in the form of environment variables would need modification
input_row.default_value: By default, application secrets are not defined. In a default OpenShift 4 cluster, the following platform objects are returned Internal Only - General Pod aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-699844b6d7-2pl8k Pod aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-6dcc794464-gnl6l Pod aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-6dcc794464-kkhr9 Deployment aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller Deployment aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller ReplicaSet aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-699844b6d7 ReplicaSet aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-777d8fbb87 ReplicaSet aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-658754b8c8 ReplicaSet aws-ebs-csi-driver-controller-6dcc794464

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access OpenShift Console:**
   - Log into the OpenShift Console as an administrator or a user with sufficient permissions.
  
2. **Navigate to the Project/Namespace:**
   - Access the relevant project/namespace where your applications are deployed.

3. **List All Resources:**
   - Use the command: `oc get all --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{.items[*].kind} {.items[*].metadata.name} {.items[*].spec.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.name}'`
   - This command retrieves all resources and checks for any environment variable references to secrets.

4. **Verify the Usage of Environment Variables:**
   - Review the output for any entries that show environment variable usage referencing secrets (look for any `secretKeyRef`).

5. **Evaluate Configuration:**
   - For each instance found using environment variables for secrets, verify if it's possible to convert the configuration to use secrets as files instead.

6. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - Ensure that any application retrieving sensitive data does so using mounted secret files instead of environment variables.

7. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Document any instances found that still use environment variables for secrets and address them according to remediation suggestions.
